title: Connected & Automated Vehicle Corridor
summary: Developing Kansas's first CAV testing corridor for real-world deployment. Assessing safety, mobility, and environmental impacts of connected infrastructure with KDOT.

title: AI-Driven Crash Prediction for Commercial Vehicles
summary: Using explainable machine learning to predict commercial motor vehicle crash severity and inform enforcement strategy. FMCSA high-priority research.

title: Adaptive & Resilient Infrastructures (ARISE)
summary: $20M NSF EPSCoR Track-1 project modeling interdependence between transportation and critical infrastructure systems for social equity.

title: Autonomous Truck Mounted Attenuator (ATMA)
summary: Exploring autonomous maintenance vehicle technology for Kansas highway work zones — evaluating cost-benefits under varying environmental conditions.

title: Wireless Dynamic Charging for EVs
summary: Analyzing whether wireless-charging pavements can generate revenue to recover highway funds — a cost-benefit analysis for Kansas.

title: Golden-Hour Identification for Rural Crashes
summary: Mapping time-critical response zones in rural Kansas to improve emergency response and reduce fatality rates in remote areas.

title: Smart Cities: Connected Signal Control
summary: Co-optimizing signal control and curbside utilization using connected vehicle data — a smart city approach for urban mobility.

title: Post-Disaster Routing & Response Planning
summary: Uncertainty-aware post-disaster routing using multi meta-heuristic optimization to enable rapid emergency response in damaged networks.

title: Automated Shared-Use Mobility Services
summary: Integrating vehicle automation, connectivity, and electrification with shared-use mobility — modeling on-demand services for communities.

title: Big Data Solutions for Mobility
summary: Leveraging connected vehicle Big Data and urban analytics for mobility insights — developed at Oak Ridge National Laboratory.
